summary: Follow-up observations from the evidence fingerprint model cleanup and closeout verification.

observations.problem: release-evidence-closeout reruns full release smoke and the full report-contract pytest wrapper even when the active change only needs generated evidence reconciliation; the wrapper took about 956 seconds in this task and was repeated several times while chasing generated artifact freshness.
observations.why_it_matters: Long serial closeout loops make small report-contract fixes expensive, encourage interruption, and blur the difference between code validation, evidence regeneration, and final release signoff.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a fingerprint-aware closeout planner that can choose quick evidence-only regeneration, targeted report-contract tests, or full release evidence based on changed source surfaces and existing test-execution-summary target fingerprints.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented release-evidence-refresh-fast as the evidence-only lane for an already-current source tree. It skips release-smoke-full, uses test-execution-summary-reuse, then runs generated-artifact-index, archive-execution-manifest-report, artifact-freshness, release-closeout-summary, and release-evidence-cohort.

observations.problem: The report-contract wrapper simultaneously validates checked-in generated artifacts and writes one of those artifacts, requiring bespoke deselections and repeated post-wrapper refresh passes.
observations.why_it_matters: Self-referential generated evidence causes confusing failures after otherwise-correct code changes and makes operators rerun broad targets to settle artifact freshness rather than validating the actual behavioral delta.
observations.automation_candidate: Split report-contract validation into a pure test lane and an evidence materialization lane, with a deterministic aggregate path that can reuse a successful prior pytest run when test target fingerprints and collect-nodeid digest match.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented test-execution-summary --reuse-if-current and the test-execution-summary-reuse Make target. The reuse path consumes an existing passing summary only when the command, target file fingerprints, structured deselections, and collect-only node id digest match.

observations.problem: The report-contract summary wrapper included broad runtime regression files such as artifact freshness, archived-run backfill, raw-registry evidence bundle, release closeout/cohort, and live report generation smoke. Per-file timing showed multiple 40-180 second files, making the wrapper exceed 15 minutes.
observations.why_it_matters: A report-contract evidence wrapper should validate the minimal contract needed for the summary artifact. Mixing full runtime regression into that wrapper makes every generated-artifact closeout pay the cost of unrelated integration coverage.
observations.automation_candidate: Split report-contract tests into a core summary lane and an explicit extended regression lane, then assert in static tests that known heavy files stay out of the core lane.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented REPORT_CONTRACT_CORE_TESTS as the default REPORT_CONTRACT_TESTS, moved the previous broad list to REPORT_CONTRACT_EXTENDED_TESTS and make report-contracts-extended, and added static guards that the heavy files remain outside the core lane.
